TestCase z40Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    int* follow_case_b = (int*)malloc(t1.n * sizeof(int));
    memcpy(follow_case_b, t1.b, t1.n * sizeof(int));
    follow_case.b = follow_case_b;
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n < 1
    if (follow_case.n < 1) {
        // No elements, nothing to modify, return replica directly
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    // Try to find elements greater than -999
    int count_greater = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] > -999) {
            count_greater++;
        }
    }
    if (count_greater == 0) {
        // No element > -999, no modification needed
        return follow_case;
    }
    
    // Select a random element index with value > -999
    // Since function should be safe and robust but no random seed provided,
    // Use a simple deterministic approach to pick the first found element > -999
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] > -999) {
            follow_case.b[i] = -999;
            break;
        }
    }

    return follow_case;
}
